briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. Welcome to the climate briefing for May 21, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Crisis Situation: Warnings and Tangible Impacts of Intensifying Climate Change
sections.content: Several reports today clearly demonstrate the severe impacts of accelerating climate change worldwide. According to multiple reports, irreversible ecosystem changes are underway, with the Earth's glaciers being declared 'extinct' or 'officially vanished' [55, 62, 79]. Furthermore, threats to food security are becoming a reality, including price increases for major agricultural products like coffee, cocoa, and olives, and rice crops facing high-temperature limits [2, 64]. Climate change is exacerbating allergy seasons, choking rivers worldwide, and causing a mass extinction crisis for plants, leading to widespread ecological crises [67, 69, 73]. In the UK, mild climates no longer exist, and plans are being made to use air conditioning to cope with heatwaves [9].

sections.title: ⚠️ Political Headwinds and Policy Obstacles: Barriers to Climate Action
sections.content: Despite the urgency of responding to climate change, significant political obstacles worldwide are hindering climate action. Even though the UN adopted a resolution urging action on climate change, the United States expressed opposition, and Russia and Iran also voted against the UN climate change resolution [4, 54, 63]. Moreover, former President Trump moved to relax regulations on refrigerants, known as major contributors to climate change, under the pretext of reducing living costs, an action that overlooks the fact that older refrigerants exacerbate climate change [1, 56, 77, 85]. Such 'Trumpism' casts a shadow over international climate efforts and risks deepening the crisis [87]. Domestically, Seoul's renewable energy self-sufficiency rate recorded the lowest in the nation at 0.6%, raising the need for policy continuity [30, 32].

sections.title: 🌍🌱 Global and Domestic Efforts to Enhance Climate Change Adaptation and Resilience
sections.content: Globally, efforts to strengthen adaptation and resilience against the threats of climate change are actively underway. Hai Phong City in Vietnam is actively responding to urban flooding caused by climate change, and four provinces in north-central Vietnam have achieved tangible results in investing in climate change-adaptive urban infrastructure [7, 10]. A European country is building a network of 'climate shelters' in preparation for heatwaves, and Dayton, USA, is investing $3.3 million in improving old homes to respond to climate change [11, 65]. Domestically, Seoul National University will host a regional and spatial analysis seminar on the theme of 'Resilience and Climate Change Adaptation: Theory and Practice,' and the National Arboretum is emphasizing the urgency of preserving 'pungnyeolji' (wind-hole areas) amidst the climate crisis [3, 12].

sections.title: 🇰🇷🌱 Domestic and International Cooperation and Policy Initiatives Towards Carbon Neutrality and Energy Transition
sections.content: Domestic and international cooperation and policy efforts for achieving carbon neutrality and energy transition are continuously expanding. The OECD announced that wealthy nations continue to exceed the $100 billion target for climate change response funds, and UN member states have agreed on legal obligations for climate change response [6, 61]. Domestically, various organizations such as the Korean Red Cross and Net Zero 2050 Climate Foundation, the Korea Chamber of Commerce and Industry and Korea Southern Power, and Konkuk University and Korea Western Power are signing MOUs to strengthen joint research and talent development for carbon neutrality, climate crisis response, and promoting energy transition [8, 15, 19]. In particular, Gyeongbuk Province is making all-out efforts to simultaneously attract large nuclear power plants and SMRs to achieve both energy security and carbon neutrality goals, and Vice Minister Lee Ho-hyun of the Ministry of Climate emphasized the establishment of an institutional foundation for energy transition towards a carbon-neutral energy mix [14, 27, 49].

sections.title: 🌱 Renewable Energy Expansion and Citizen Participation: Driving Force for a Sustainable Future
sections.content: Specific projects for expanding renewable energy are being promoted, and public support for them is also very high. Sinan County is demonstrating its value as a regional survival strategy by proposing a solution to the problem of population decline through its 'Sunshine Pension' system, which utilizes renewable energy [29, 34, 40]. Gwangmyeong City is making efforts to disseminate renewable energy, including recruiting households and buildings wishing to install renewable energy and conducting citizen demand surveys [28, 38, 43]. Plans have been announced to significantly increase renewable energy, primarily solar power, by 2030, and Hanwha Q CELLS has supplied domestically produced modules for the largest solar power project in Korea, signaling the revitalization of the market [39, 45, 47, 48]. Above all, 82% of the public supports the expansion of renewable energy, indicating a very high social consensus on sustainable energy transition [52].

briefing.closing: This concludes today's climate briefing for May 21, 2026. Thank you.
